C-GENPARAMS-RESOLVED.decision: Adopt the OFFICIAL Evaluation page gen params as authoritative for local CV: temperature=0.0 (greedy), max_tokens=7680, top_p=1.0, max_model_len=8192, max_lora_rank=32. Winner-notebook evaluate() defaults (1.0/3584/4096) are NON-authoritative.
C-GENPARAMS-RESOLVED.rationale: Host scores greedily; a CV measured at temp=1.0 sampling does not predict the leaderboard. temp 1.0->0.0 is the key fix.

D-AUDIT-2026-05-30.decision: Pause new feature work; land the 6 audit-corrections + 5 seeded tasks before adding capability.
D-AUDIT-2026-05-30.rationale: Audit found 5 live defects (3 fixed mid-pass), 6 invalidated assumptions, 0 end-to-end runs. Infrastructure-complete but execution-untriggered. Adding features now compounds cross-PR invariant drift the audit identified.

D-CATEGORIES-6.decision: train.csv = EXACTLY 6 categories: gravity, numeral, unit_conversion, bit_manipulation, cipher, equation_numeric. DROP cryptarithm/cryptarithm_guess/select2reason from the corpus (not in test distribution). Build operator-VERIFIED solvers for the 3 real-but-synthetic categories (bit_manipulation=binary, cipher=word-substitution, equation_numeric=SYMBOLIC transform) by running on classified real rows and verifying vs gold.
D-CATEGORIES-6.rationale: Classifier over 9500 rows: 6 signatures partition the set exactly; 0 cryptarithm/?-pattern rows. Winner's 9-category analysis was a different dataset version.

D-EXPLAN-001.decision: Lock experimentation plan (Phase A done, B->C->D->E->F; E-007 cryptarithm_guess prioritized).
D-EXPLAN-001.rationale: Cross-validated against winner repo (R13), discussion deltas (R11b), 18 ledger hypotheses. See EXPERIMENTATION_PLAN.md.

D-V13-VERIFIED.decision: Adopt v13 (3802 rows, 100% verified, 5 cats, equation held out) as the training corpus for the improved run, replacing v12. Attach sai1881/nemotron-cot-corpus-v13 to the notebook (swap out v12) before the post-baseline run.
D-V13-VERIFIED.rationale: v13 is 100% train.csv-matched guaranteed-correct CoT; v12 was 60% synthetic/fabricated incl 3 categories absent from train.csv. Verified corpus should raise CV/LB and remove wrong-reasoning training.

E-001-corpus-v1.notes: Assemble corpus.jsonl from 7 landed solvers + verified CoT; stratified train/dev; winner build_datum mask convention.

E-002-baseline-rank32.notes: Winner-faithful baseline. rank=32, 1 epoch, step-linear LR 2e-5->1e-5, MLP+attention(+unembed) targets. ALPHA NOTE (corrected 2026-05-31): winner used tinker framework (create_lora_training_client_async rank=32, train_mlp/attn/unembed) which abstracts lora_alpha away — there is NO primary-source alpha=64. Baseline uses the official demo's alpha=16 (scaling 0.5); alpha is a POST-baseline ablation axis (16 vs 32 vs 64). Goal ~0.85.

E-006-select2reason.notes: Full corpus -> top-utility filtered (~30 percent retained).

E-007-cryptarithm-guess.notes: Add cryptarithm_guess CoT to corpus (winner skipped). Highest-confidence direct LB.

E-009-synthesis.notes: Combine winning ablation choices into final config. Train + submit (gated).

E-PRECOND-mamba-targets.notes: Smoke run (10 steps) on 2xT4 4-bit with explicit Mamba+attention targets. Validates no MoE-scan hang.

H-1EPOCH.statement: 1-epoch SFT is sufficient given high-quality CoT; more epochs over-fit on synthetic patterns.

H-AUTO-RESCUE.expected_effect: Removes one human-in-the-loop step per failure. Observed rate: 1/11 dispatches in the RUN-20260530-124519 fanout. At scale (50-100 sessions/day expected near deadline), this saves ~5-10 manual rescues/day.
H-AUTO-RESCUE.how_to_test: Mock a session-state response with state=COMPLETED + pr_url=None and an activities response containing a valid unidiff. Run poll_in_flight - verify (1) a rescue branch is created, (2) patch applies cleanly, (3) pytest is invoked, (4) merge to main happens via the same github_ops.merge_pr code path, (5) the session is cleared. Also test the failure case where pytest fails: rescue branch is left in place but main is NOT modified.
H-AUTO-RESCUE.statement: Tick RUN-20260530-131519 surfaced a real failure mode: session 290296703496810863 emitted sessionCompleted with a complete unidiff in its artifact (tools/package_submission.py + tests/test_package_submission.py) but never opened a GitHub PR. The operator had to manually extract the patch, apply it to a rescue branch, run pytest, and merge to main. Adding an operator-side auto-rescue path inside orchestrator/loop.py.poll_in_flight - when a session returns state=COMPLETED with pr_url=None, fetch /sessions/<sid>/activities, find the last sessionCompleted artifact gitPatch, apply it to a fresh branch, run pytest -q, merge to main via the existing R-007 force-merge path - eliminates this whole manual step.
H-AUTO-RESCUE.title: Auto-rescue Jules sessions that fire sessionCompleted with a full patch but no PR (eliminates manual operator merges)

H-BIT-NICKNAME.statement: Bit-manipulation problems use nickname encodings (e.g. "the integer formed by bytes…"); a robust regex extractor across all 1602 bit_manipulation rows yields 100% solver coverage.

H-CHAT-TEMPLATE.expected_effect: Catches a class of silent failures that would only manifest as 'CV looks 0.0 on a well-trained adapter' - very hard to debug after the fact.
H-CHAT-TEMPLATE.how_to_test: Create a 10-prompt fixture with known correct host rendering (captured from Nemotron's HF tokenizer with default chat_template + reasoning_on system message). Run vllm_eval.py in render-only mode against the same fixture - assert byte-equality of every rendered prompt. Test fails if our template diverges.
H-CHAT-TEMPLATE.statement: Discussions repeatedly note that the host scoring runs inference with a SPECIFIC chat template (Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16 default + reasoning_on header). If our LoRA adapter is trained or evaluated under a different template (e.g. our eval/vllm_eval.py uses ChatML where host uses Nemotron's native), every output is a few tokens off and \boxed{} extraction may miss. Adding eval/test_chat_template_parity.py that loads the official tokenizer's apply_chat_template on a known fixture and asserts byte-equality vs the harness's rendered prompt prevents this.
H-CHAT-TEMPLATE.title: Chat-template parity test against the host inference prompt eliminates silent-failure submissions

H-CIPHER-100.expected_effect: +0.16 LB from solving all 1576 cipher rows in train + matching test distribution
H-CIPHER-100.statement: Cipher-category problems reduce to substitution+offset and admit a 100% deterministic solver.

H-CIPHER-DICT.expected_effect: Converts cipher from synthetic to verified; meaningful per-category CV gain.
H-CIPHER-DICT.statement: Completing the substitution via a comprehensive English wordlist (pattern + known-letter constraints) lifts cipher solver exact-match from ~0% to >=60% on real rows, yielding verified cipher CoT.

H-CRYPTARITHM.statement: Cryptarithm-deduce problems are uniquely solvable letter-digit assignment problems; constraint propagation (or python-constraint / Z3) yields the exact answer.

H-CRYPTARITHM-GUESS.expected_effect: +0.01-0.03 LB direct from a category the winner never trained on.
H-CRYPTARITHM-GUESS.statement: Winner skipped cryptarithm_guess; adding a solver + verified CoT for it gives direct LB headroom (164 train rows).

H-CURRICULUM.expected_effect: +0.01-0.03 LB vs random ordering, free improvement
H-CURRICULUM.statement: Difficulty-ordered curriculum SFT (easy→hard within each category) generalizes better than random shuffle on the hidden test set.

H-DEEP-ANALYSIS-XLSX.expected_effect: Decision quality. Replaces 'operator skim 1MB of markdown' with 'operator reads 5-sheet xlsx with ranked experiments + code-change pseudocode for each.'
H-DEEP-ANALYSIS-XLSX.how_to_test: 4 workbooks materialize within ~6h Jules-wall-clock; next code push cites a specific xlsx row.
H-DEEP-ANALYSIS-XLSX.statement: Per-source markdown is hard to compare/rank. Structured Excel sheets with the same column vocabulary across sources (winner code, 217 discussions, community, tabs) make the operator's next-experiment decision provable: rank by (expected_cv_delta / effort), pick the top, push it.
H-DEEP-ANALYSIS-XLSX.title: Structured xlsx synthesis of every scraped source produces the highest-quality input for next code push

H-DQ.statement: DATA QUALITY is the dominant lever: per-category deterministic solvers + verified  oxed{} CoT corpora beat fancier training regimes.

H-EQ-FALLBACK.statement: Default to absolute-difference fallback in equation_numeric solvers when no rule is found; captures edge cases.

H-EQ-SYMPY.statement: Equation-numeric problems are solvable with SymPy linear/quadratic solvers; algebraic CoT is reproducible.

H-FINAL-PREP-PARALLEL.how_to_test: All 4 Jules ship within ~3h Jules wall-clock; v23 produces first real CV on next browser run; ablation_configs.py exposes 6 dicts.
H-FINAL-PREP-PARALLEL.statement: 4 Jules workers (corpus-scale 9cat, cv-fixture 9cat, host-template-parity, ablation-configs) + operator-side notebook v23 (chat template + reasoning_on + full corpus) + corpus-uploader tool close all 10 gaps from the readiness audit in parallel; once landed, the operator can run baseline + 5 ablations within 3 days.
H-FINAL-PREP-PARALLEL.title: Parallel close of all 10 gaps unblocks final-quality training within 24h

H-GENPARITY.expected_effect: CV becomes a reliable submit gate; reduces wasted submissions on CV that doesn't transfer.
H-GENPARITY.statement: Evaluating local CV with host-exact gen params (temp=0.0 greedy, max_tokens=7680, max_model_len=8192) yields a CV that tracks the leaderboard within a small constant offset; sampling-based CV (temp=1.0) does not.

H-GPU-DISPATCH.expected_effect: Removes the only manual hand-off in the loop; enables the operator's 30-min cron to also drive Kaggle GPU runs autonomously; unlocks the full E-002 -> E-003..E-009 ablation fanout to run end-to-end.
H-GPU-DISPATCH.how_to_test: After E-002 baseline kernel PR merges, run a single tick - confirm gpu_dispatch auto-fires, kernel-push returns a Kaggle kernel URL, kernel-status terminalizes, kernel-output pulls cv_score.json + adapter/ into experiments/<exp-id>/, and the operator's add_metric op records the new cv into best_cv if better.
H-GPU-DISPATCH.statement: Today the loop stops at 'kernel authored by Jules and merged to main' - the actual Kaggle GPU run is a manual operator action. Adding a gpu_dispatch ledger op + a tools/run_kernel.py runner (kernel-push -> poll kernel-status -> kernel-output) lets the operator's owner-role automatically promote a merged experiment with status=READY_FOR_GPU into a live Kaggle kernel run and capture cv_score.json + adapter dir into the ledger, completing the train->validate loop.
H-GPU-DISPATCH.title: Wiring gpu_dispatch into the loop closes the SDLC training cycle end-to-end (Jules authors kernel -> operator pushes -> kernel runs on 2xT4 -> outputs ingested as cv_score)

H-GRAVITY-100.expected_effect: +0.17 LB from solving all 1597 gravity rows (16.8% of train)
H-GRAVITY-100.statement: Gravity-category problems (free-fall/projectile/orbital mechanics) reduce to closed-form physics formulas and admit a deterministic solver.

H-LOGPROB-FILTER.statement: Filtering out examples whose base-model min-logprob > -0.05 prevents over-tuning on easy examples and improves accuracy on hard rules.

H-LORA32.statement: LoRA rank 32 / alpha 64 SFT (winner config) is the right anchor; deviations should justify themselves with CV evidence.

H-MAMBA-TARGETS.expected_effect: Unblocks training on free GPU. Critical precondition.
H-MAMBA-TARGETS.statement: Explicit Mamba mixer LoRA targeting (mixer.in_proj, mixer.out_proj) + attention-only avoids the MoE expert-scan hang and enables training on 2xT4 4-bit.

H-NORL.statement: RL / distillation are NOT needed; pure SFT on verified CoT reaches top-LB.

H-NUMERAL-100.statement: Numeral-system problems (base conversions, radix arithmetic, novel digit systems) are deterministic given the rules in the prompt.

H-PARALLEL-RESEARCH.expected_effect: Decouples campaign throughput from the GPU bottleneck. Each research task produces a concrete file that influences the next code push. Most impactful: the CoT corpus (winner's 0.65->0.85 lever).
H-PARALLEL-RESEARCH.how_to_test: All 4 tasks ship files within ~2h; next code push uses findings from at least 1 of them.
H-PARALLEL-RESEARCH.statement: While the GPU iteration is single-threaded (we wait on browser Save&Run All), 4 parallel Jules sessions can mine top notebooks, dig through 200+ discussions, synthesize CoT corpus, and gap-analyze vs winner — all CPU-only, all producing concrete analysis/data files the operator reads on next cycle to inform the next code push and the next training experiment.
H-PARALLEL-RESEARCH.title: Parallel CPU-only research while GPU is blocked accelerates the loop without using submit-cap

H-RANK16.statement: LoRA rank 16 (half the cap) reaches within 0.005 of rank-32 with half the parameters; ablation worth running for compute efficiency.

H-SELECT2REASON.expected_effect: ~equal LB at half cost; may slightly improve generalization.
H-SELECT2REASON.statement: Select2Reason-style curation (drop low-utility / repetitive traces) matches baseline accuracy at ~30 percent of cost.

H-SUBMIT-GATE.expected_effect: Saves >=1 wasted submit/day in practice (real cap is 5/day, 2 reserved finals); makes the unsupervised auto-merge safer because broken-adapter PRs cannot accidentally trigger a submit.
H-SUBMIT-GATE.how_to_test: Inject a deliberately broken adapter (rank=64) and confirm gate fails; inject an adapter that outputs no \boxed{} and confirm gate fails; pass a valid baseline adapter and confirm gate returns 0.
H-SUBMIT-GATE.statement: A tools/validate_adapter.py that (a) loads base+adapter via peft, (b) asserts the adapter_config rank <= 32, (c) runs 5 sample prompts through the adapter and confirms each output contains \boxed{...}, and (d) returns 0 only on full pass - used as a precondition by both the dispatcher submission_proposal flow and the operator submit gate - eliminates wasted submits on adapters that fail to load, exceed rank, or omit the required answer format.
H-SUBMIT-GATE.title: Pre-submit adapter-validation gate (loadability + rank<=32 + boxed-output smoke) prevents wasting submits on broken adapters

H-UNIT-CONV-100.statement: Unit-conversion problems decompose into a directed graph of conversion factors; a Floyd-Warshall closure over the parsed factors yields exact answers.

H-VERIFIED-CORPUS.expected_effect: Higher and more faithful CV; removes risk of training on wrong synthetic reasoning.
H-VERIFIED-CORPUS.statement: Replacing the 2395 synthetic rows (6 categories) with train.csv-VERIFIED solver output raises CV/LB vs the v12 hybrid, because verified rows guarantee correct CoT and match the host test distribution (same generator as train.csv).

H-VLLM-EVAL.expected_effect: Per technique-backlog #2 (effort:M, expected_gain:HIGH); enables every other iteration and prevents wasted submits against the 5/day hard cap; mirrors winner dashboards.
H-VLLM-EVAL.how_to_test: Run harness on the winner-faithful baseline adapter (from E-002 once it lands), record cv_score.json, confirm metric reproduces across 3 reruns within +/-0.5pp; then validate gate refuses to forward a deliberately-broken adapter to submit.
H-VLLM-EVAL.statement: Building eval/vllm_eval.py that loads (base + adapter), runs inference with temperature=0.0/top_p=1.0/max_tokens=7680/max_model_len=8192/max_lora_rank=32 and extracts \boxed{...} for exact (and +/-1e-2 numeric) match against a held-out CV slice gives us a reproducible local CV metric that the operator-side submit gate can compare against best_cv, preventing burned submit slots on duplicates and surfacing regressions before they hit the leaderboard.
H-VLLM-EVAL.title: Local vLLM CV/eval harness mirroring exact host inference params unlocks beats-best-CV submit gate

TASK-4.0.gpu: no  (authoring only — training runs on the GPU executor)
TASK-4.0.title: Author the QLoRA training script + Kaggle kernel

TASK-ABLATION-configs.spec: Create a single Python module that yields 5 ablation configs as dicts (one per knob), each one compatible with our notebook's tunables (LORA_RANK, MAX_TRAIN, REASONING_ON, EPOCHS, etc). Place at competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/experiments/ablation_configs.py exposing CONFIGS = {'baseline':..., 'rank16':..., '2epoch':..., 'curriculum':..., 'select2reason':..., 'logprob_filter':...}}. For each, include: (a) the parameter delta from baseline (e.g. rank=16 instead of 32), (b) one-line hypothesis, (c) source citation (winner repo file:line or discussion id). Also write experiments/README.md explaining how the operator applies one config to the notebook via save_notebook (search/replace the tunable block). Add tests/test_ablation_configs.py asserting all 6 keys exist and each has the required fields. Acceptance: 6 configs total (baseline + 5); each has rank, alpha, epochs, lr, target_modules, hypothesis, source; pytest -q green.
TASK-ABLATION-configs.title: Pre-build 5 ablation config JSONs (rank16, 2epoch, curriculum, select2reason, logprob_filter)

TASK-CORPUS-curation.acceptance_criteria: curate() returns kept subset + drop report; deterministic; unit test on inline fixture passes; no source mutation.
TASK-CORPUS-curation.definition_of_done: One PR adding curation module + test under allowed_area; tests pass.
TASK-CORPUS-curation.spec: Implement a Select2Reason-style curation filter over an existing corpus.jsonl (rows have prompt/completion/category). Goal: quality over quantity - keep the highest-utility traces, drop near-duplicate/repetitive ones, balance per category. Implement curate(rows:list[dict], per_cat_cap:int=None)->(kept, dropped, report): group by (category, normalized-template signature), prefer longer/more-informative CoT, down-sample near-duplicates, and produce a report dict with kept/dropped counts per category and the selection rationale. Do NOT mutate or delete source files - read input path, write curated output + report JSON to your area. This needs NO train.csv. Include unit tests on a small synthetic in-repo fixture (build a tiny list of rows inline).
TASK-CORPUS-curation.title: Select2Reason corpus curation filter

TASK-CORPUS-scale-9cat.spec: Read existing data/corpus/v2/corpus.jsonl (398 rows, 4 cats) and references/analysis-winner.md + winner-reasoning.py.md + winner-augmentation.py.md + winner-corpus.py.md. Build competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/data/corpus/v4/corpus.jsonl with: (a) 9 categories covered: bit_manipulation, cipher, gravity, numeral, cryptarithm, cryptarithm_guess, equation_numeric, unit_conversion, select2reason. (b) >= 300 rows per category (3000+ total). (c) Use per-category deterministic Python solvers (NO LLM/API), mirror winner's reasoning.py logic. (d) Apply the 5 winner augmenters (paraphrase, perturbation, etc) per augmentation.py. (e) HARD verification: each row's completion must end with \boxed{X} where X exactly equals or numerically matches the original answer; reject mismatched rows; do NOT mark verified=True falsely. (f) Write data/corpus/v4/quality_report.json with per-category {generated, verified, rejected, samples}. (g) Add tests/test_corpus_v4_quality.py asserting >= 3000 rows, >= 95% verified, all 9 cats present. Acceptance: pytest -q green; quality report shows >= 95% verified for every category.
TASK-CORPUS-scale-9cat.title: Scale CoT corpus to 3K+ rows across ALL 9 host categories (winner-grade volume)

TASK-DATA-cot-corpus-v1.spec: This is technique-backlog #1: the largest single CV-lever. Read references/analysis-winner.md + winner-reasoning.py.md + winner-corpus.py.md + winner-augmentation.py.md. Build competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/data/corpus/cot_v1.jsonl by: (a) loading data/raw/train.csv (run tools/download_competition_data.py if missing), (b) classifying each row into one of the 9 host categories using data/curation/classify.py, (c) for each row generating a step-by-step \boxed{} chain-of-thought using a per-category deterministic solver (Python, NO LLM/API calls). Cover at least 6 of the 9 categories (equation_numeric, cryptarithm, cipher, bit_manipulation, numeral, gravity priorities). Output schema per line: {id, category, prompt, cot_completion, verified:bool}. Target: >=500 verified-CoT rows. Add tests/test_cot_corpus_v1.py asserting each output row has \boxed{answer} matching the original train.csv answer (verification gate). Acceptance: corpus.jsonl exists with >=500 rows >=90 percent verified across >=6 categories; pytest -q green.
TASK-DATA-cot-corpus-v1.title: Build CoT corpus from train.csv using winner's deterministic solver pattern (the 0.65->0.85 lever)

TASK-DATA-solve-real-rows.notes: Using classify.py output: for bit_manipulation(binary), cipher(word-sub), equation_numeric(symbolic transform) run/extend the deterministic solvers on the REAL classified rows, verify each \boxed{} vs gold, emit verified corpus. Assemble v13 = 3 verified base cats (gravity/numeral/unit_conv) + 3 newly-verified (bit/cipher/equation), DROP fabricated cats. Target: maximize verified coverage of the full 9500.
TASK-DATA-solve-real-rows.title: Run solvers on REAL train.csv rows -> verified corpus v13

TASK-DATA-train-classifier.notes: OPERATOR-ONLY (Jules VMs lack train.csv). Classify all 9500 train.csv rows into the 9 categories by prompt pattern. Seed the 3 verified categories via exact-prompt membership from v6/v12 matched rows; pattern-match the other 6. Output id->category map + per-category counts. Unlocks running solvers on REAL rows to convert 6 synthetic categories -> verified, and exploits the full 9500 set (vs ~4k now).
TASK-DATA-train-classifier.title: train.csv category classifier (operator-owned, needs raw data)

TASK-E-PRECOND-mamba-targets-smoke.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-smoke-mamba/. Change LoRA target_modules to the EXPLICIT list ['mixer.in_proj','mixer.out_proj','q_proj','k_proj','v_proj','o_proj'] (NOT a regex). Set max_steps=10 (smoke only). Keep rank=32, alpha=64, 4-bit. Update kernel-metadata.json. The goal is to prove peft.get_peft_model() does NOT hang on the MoE expert scan on 2xT4. Offline test that asserts target_modules is a list of literal strings (no regex). Document the result expected: training loop starts within 30s of model load.
TASK-E-PRECOND-mamba-targets-smoke.title: Smoke-train kernel (10 steps) validating Mamba mixer LoRA targeting

TASK-E001-corpus-v1.spec: Build data/corpus/v1/build_corpus.py that walks the 7+ landed solvers in data/solvers/<category>/, calls each to generate verified prompt+CoT+answer triples, writes corpus.jsonl in the winner's build_datum format (tokens, mask aligned per references/analysis-winner-training-internals.md): mask=1 ONLY on the CoT-reasoning + boxed-answer span, mask=0 on the prompt. Stratified 90/10 train/dev split keeping category frequencies. Emit corpus_index.jsonl listing per-row id/category/token_counts. Tests: format validation, mask correctness, stratification quality.
TASK-E001-corpus-v1.title: Assemble corpus.jsonl v1 from landed solvers (winner build_datum convention)

TASK-E002-SMOKE-RUN.acceptance_criteria: Kernel completes; outputs pulled; runtime <10 min; metrics logged.
TASK-E002-SMOKE-RUN.definition_of_done: Result under experiments/SMOKE-001/; cv recorded via update_gpu_run.
TASK-E002-SMOKE-RUN.spec: Push smoke-mamba kernel with reduced hyperparams (rank=4, max_steps=20, 100 rows). Validate full chain: kernel-push -> status=complete -> output-pull -> cv_score.json + adapter_config.json present. Do NOT submit. Steelman counter-arg 8.
TASK-E002-SMOKE-RUN.title: Kaggle GPU smoke run: 5-min rank=4 max_steps=20 100-row preflight

TASK-E002-baseline-kernel.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-baseline-e002/. Final config: LoRA rank=32, alpha=64, 1 epoch, LinearDecayLRSchedule 2e-5 -> 1e-5 (per winner-lr_schedule.py), AdamW weight_decay=0.01, BF16 mixed precision, Mamba+attention target list from E-PRECOND, grad-accum for the largest stable micro-batch on 2xT4 4-bit, eval at end of epoch using eval/cv.py, write adapter/ + cv_score.json on success. Loss objective: maximize min log-prob over the masked CoT+answer span per winner-loss_config.py (CrossEntropyLossConfig baseline; no clipping/branch-weighting). Reject if smoke fails. Offline test that asserts rank<=32 and 1 epoch.
TASK-E002-baseline-kernel.title: Finalize winner-faithful baseline training kernel (rank32/alpha64/1-epoch/LinearDecay)

TASK-OPS-auto-rescue.spec: Extend orchestrator/loop.py.poll_in_flight: when a session response has state=COMPLETED AND pr_url is empty, before clearing the session: (1) GET https://jules.googleapis.com/v1alpha/sessions/<sid>/activities (use the existing JulesClient or jules_pool routing). (2) Find the last activity with sessionCompleted and a non-empty artifact.changeSet.gitPatch.unidiffPatch; if none found, mark the session FAILED and skip rescue. (3) Create a temp rescue branch off main: 'operator-rescue/<sid>-<short_ts>'. (4) Write the patch to a temp file and run `git apply --whitespace=nowarn <patchfile>`. If apply fails, mark FAILED and skip. (5) Run pytest -q with subprocess.run; capture rc. If rc != 0, leave the rescue branch and mark the session FAILED with a rescue_failed note. (6) On rc==0: commit the staged files with message 'rescue: <task_id> from session <sid>'; switch to main; merge --no-ff with the same -X theirs strategy used by github_ops.merge_pr; scan diff for secrets (same regex KGAT_/AQ.Ab/sk-ant-); on secret leak, abort and mark FAILED. (7) On success: mark the task DONE and clear the session - emit the ops via the existing StatePatch flow, not a side effect. Add tests/test_auto_rescue.py covering: happy path (rescue + merge + DONE), pytest-fails path (rescue branch present, main untouched, FAILED), patch-fails path (FAILED, no branch), no-artifact path (FAILED, no branch), secret-leak path (FAILED, no merge). Acceptance: pytest -q green; happy path produces a real merge commit in a test git repo.
TASK-OPS-auto-rescue.title: Add auto-rescue path to poll_in_flight (COMPLETED-without-PR -> extract patch + pytest + merge)

TASK-SOLVER-cipher.spec: Write a pure-Python deterministic solver for the cipher category. The prompt gives example pairs 'ciphertext -> plaintext' (e.g. 'ucoov pwgtfyoqg' -> 'queen discovers') and then 'decrypt: <ciphertext>'. Algorithm: align each ciphertext word with its plaintext word to build a consistent char->char substitution map across ALL examples; if conflicts arise prefer the most frequent mapping; apply the map to the target ciphertext; emit a CONCISE CoT showing the mapping table + application, ending in \boxed{plaintext}. IMPORTANT: no train.csv on the VM (gitignored) - work only from in-prompt examples. Provide solve(prompt)->str and build_rows(prompts)->list[dict] with category='cipher'. Include >=6 inline unit tests with hand-verified answers (operator runs it against local train.csv afterward). Stdlib-only.
TASK-SOLVER-cipher.title: Substitution-cipher CoT solver

TASK-SUBMIT-gate-decision.spec: Add orchestrator/submit_gate.py module with function decide_submit(state, kaggle_lite) -> {action:'submit'|'skip', reason:str, candidate:{experiment_id, blob_token, cv_score} | None}. Behavior: (1) Find candidate experiments with status=READY_FOR_SUBMIT + recorded cv_score + blob_token. (2) Skip unless cv_score > state['best_cv'] by at least a configurable margin (default 0.0). (3) Skip unless today's auto-submit count < MAX_AUTO_SUBMITS_PER_DAY (env, default 3). (4) Skip unless live kaggle_lite.submissions(comp) cap-remaining >= 1 + 2 (reserve 2 finals). (5) Wire orchestrator/loop.py.apply_decision: when decide_submit returns submit, call tools.kaggle_lite.competition_submit and emit record_submission op + bump submits_by_day. (6) Add tests/test_submit_gate.py covering each skip branch + the happy submit path (mock kaggle_lite). Acceptance: pytest -q green; happy path emits submit decision + records; all 4 skip branches return correct reason strings.
